text:
I once knew a rabbit named Chuck who lived deep in the forest. Chuck had a peculiar talent—he could disappear for days at a time and return without explanation. His friends would always try to find him, but he'd always just hop away into the woods, leaving behind nothing but a trail of paw prints.

Chuck always came back home, somehow managing to keep his own disappearing acts secret. One time when he disappeared into the forest for what seemed like an entire month, he returned with an old cloak draped over his shoulders. Everyone cheered for him.

That's when Chuck learned he could turn into a fine thief. With a cloak that melded into the night, he could move unseen through the forest. He never stole anything of value; he just enjoyed surprising his friends. One night, he decided to sneak into their cozy barn to look at their prized goose eggs.

As Chuck approached, the eggs seemed too tempting to leave unnoticed. But he remembered his friends' fear of birds. Instead of eating
